search_terms_used: carbohydrate fueling during running marathon systematic review
glucose fructose oxidation running endurance exogenous carbohydrate
gut training carbohydrate endurance runners tolerance
carbohydrate periodization train low compete high endurance review
coverage_notes: Strong coverage of in-exercise fueling (systematic review + narrative reviews), glucose-fructose multiple transportable carbohydrate research (critical review + foundational RCT + applied hydrogel RCT), gut training (RCT), carbohydrate periodization (theoretical framework + meta-analysis), glycogen metabolism (review), and practical marathon fueling guidelines. Includes 1 systematic review, 1 meta-analysis, 1 foundational RCT, 1 applied RCT, and 6 reviews spanning 2005-2022. Mix of cycling and running populations. Gap: no dedicated carbo-loading protocol RCT included (covered within reviews).

papers.title: Systematic review: Carbohydrate supplementation on exercise performance or capacity of varying durations
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 61 studies examining carbohydrate supplementation effects on exercise performance across varying durations.

papers.title: A step towards personalized sports nutrition: carbohydrate intake during exercise
papers.notes: Proposed updated carbohydrate intake guidelines stratified by exercise duration and intensity, incorporating multiple transportable carbohydrate research.

papers.title: The use of carbohydrates during exercise as an ergogenic aid
papers.notes: Reviewed evidence for carbohydrate ingestion as ergogenic aid during prolonged exercise, including dose-response and multiple transportable carbohydrate recommendations.

papers.title: Carbohydrates for training and competition
papers.notes: Provided updated guidelines for carbohydrate intake during training and competition, distinguishing high vs low carbohydrate availability strategies.

papers.title: Fructose-Glucose Composite Carbohydrates and Endurance Performance: Critical Review and Future Perspectives
papers.notes: Critically reviewed 14 studies on fructose-glucose composite beverages examining optimal ratios, oxidation rates, and endurance performance effects.

papers.title: High rates of exogenous carbohydrate oxidation from a mixture of glucose and fructose ingested during prolonged cycling exercise
papers.notes: Demonstrated that co-ingesting glucose and fructose at high rates during cycling achieved exogenous carbohydrate oxidation rates of ~1.75 g/min.

papers.title: Glucose and Fructose Hydrogel Enhances Running Performance, Exogenous Carbohydrate Oxidation, and Gastrointestinal Tolerance
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: Tested a glucose-fructose hydrogel formulation vs standard solution in trained male runners, finding 2.1% faster time-trial performance with the hydrogel.

papers.title: Gut-training: the impact of two weeks repetitive gut-challenge during exercise on gastrointestinal status, glucose availability, fuel kinetics, and running performance
papers.notes: Investigated a 2-week gut-training protocol in endurance runners, measuring effects on GI tolerance, glucose availability, and running performance.

papers.title: Fuel for the Work Required: A Theoretical Framework for Carbohydrate Periodization and the Glycogen Threshold Hypothesis
papers.notes: Proposed the glycogen threshold hypothesis as a theoretical framework for carbohydrate periodization in endurance training.

papers.title: Performance effects of periodized carbohydrate restriction in endurance trained athletes - a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
papers.notes: Conducted a meta-analysis of 9 studies examining whether periodized carbohydrate restriction enhances endurance performance compared to normal carbohydrate availability.
